key_insight: The paper adds a Lagrange-multiplier correction to evolving surface finite element methods so mean curvature flow stays structure-preserving and area-decreasing at each time step.
engineering_relevance: This gives a stable, high-order way to simulate curvature-driven surface evolution with provable error bounds, which is useful for geometry processing and interface evolution in computational mechanics.
method: Uses a solution-driven ESFEM formulation based on Huisken-type evolution equations for the normal vector and mean curvature.
Applies linearly implicit BDF schemes for geometric variables and implicit Adams updates for nodal positions.
Includes optional minimal-deformation-rate tangential motion while preserving discrete area decay.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal-order H1 error bounds of order h^k+τ^q and numerical sphere tests that match the predicted convergence rates with only small overhead.
watch_item: The strongest guarantees rely on stronger regularity assumptions, and practical performance still depends on accurate discrete Lagrange-multiplier solves and Newton iteration convergence.
paper_title: Structure-preserving Lagrange-multiplier methods for mean curvature flow and their error bounds
